title: ResCon Outbound Pipeline
description:
Seeding-ready workflows for the GPR ResCon outbound campaign.
Contains only the workflows in the 99_rescon_outbound_scheduled
dependency tree — no investigation atoms, no baselines.

Dependency tree:
  orchestrator (rescon_outbound_scheduled)
  ├── pre-call-pipeline
  │   ├── _atoms/soql-open-cases
  │   └── _atoms/contact-for-account
  ├── [batch_call] (built-in node type)
  └── post-call-pipeline
      ├── _atoms/close-case-confirmed
      └── _atoms/close-case-no-contact

For investigation/audit workflows (describe_*, investigate_*, baselines)
see ../account-gpr/.

scenarios.name: orchestrator
scenarios.description:
End-to-end ResCon outbound pipeline intended to run on a Temporal Schedule
(see ``apps/service/scripts/apply_schedules.py``). No manual trigger_input
beyond ``phone_number_id`` + ``zen_workflow_id`` — eligible contacts come
from Salesforce, so rows are NOT user-supplied.

Pipeline:
  1. subflow    → pre-call-pipeline (Salesforce eligibility pipeline) → rows
  2. transform  → shape rows into ``{contacts}`` envelope for batch_call
  3. batch_call → fan-out dials (empty-contacts guard is handled inside
                  BatchCallWorkflow — it short-circuits to a zero-result
                  envelope, so no explicit guard node is needed here)
  4. subflow    → post-call-pipeline (SF VoiceCall + Case close per row,
                  branching on call outcome: confirmed vs no-contact)
  5. response   → summary counts surfaced to the run detail page

Co-located with pre-call-pipeline so subflow resolution stays sibling-by-name
(the engine's subflow handler rejects cross-suite filesystem refs).

scenarios.name: post-call-pipeline
scenarios.description:
End-to-end post-call writeback composite. Consumes batch_call's per-row
result envelope from the orchestrator, branches per row on call outcome,
and dispatches to the correct atom:

  reservation_confirmed=true           -> _atoms/close-case-confirmed
  not answered (no_answer/busy/failed) -> _atoms/close-case-no-contact
  answered but not confirmed           -> skipped (no case closure)
    (callback_requested, declined + transferred, wrong number)

Branching uses collected_variables from the enrichment signal, not just
the binary success flag. An answered call where the owner declined or
requested a callback should NOT be closed as "RC: Confirmed".

Pipeline:
  1. transform -> normalize batch_call results: compute outcome using
                 collected_variables, split into closeable + skipped
  2. loop      -> for each closeable result, dispatch to the correct atom
  3. transform -> roll up per-row outcomes into a single summary
  4. response  -> return aggregate counts + per-row branch decisions

scenarios.name: pre-call-pipeline
scenarios.description:
End-to-end pre-call pipeline. Inputs nothing.

Pipeline:
  01 SOQL query (filters 1..5 enforced at SF)
    -> dedupe by AccountId (group each owner's cases; preserve earliest
      future check-in as primary)
    -> Phase 1 filter: keep only accounts with count(cases) == 1
    -> loop over single-date accounts
        |-- fetch contact for account
        +-- shape eligible-callable row
    -> join with primary case context
    -> response

Phase 1 single-date filter keeps only accounts that have a single open
date in the future so that we don't have to handle multiple dates.
Phase 2 will replace this with a chronological loop across an owner's
future dates.

scenarios.name: close-case-confirmed
scenarios.description:
Post-call writeback for the ANSWERED / Confirmed branch.

Mirrors the legacy post-call logging flow — the positive
(outcome=completed) path:

  1. POST /sobjects/VoiceCall  -> create VoiceCall record linked to the
     Case + Account, with all wrap-up fields (call source, disposition,
     resort, summary, transcript, detail).
  2. PATCH /sobjects/Case/{id} -> flip Case to Closed with
     Resolution_Code__c = "RC: Confirmed" and
     Case_Resolution_Detail_v2__c = "RC: Will Use".

Both calls are made via Nango Proxy with the existing salesforce
connection (sibling pattern with soql-open-cases). No conditional logic
inside this atom — the branch decision is handled one level up in
../post-call-pipeline/.

Field surface mirrors the production VoiceCall body verbatim so the
resulting SF record is equivalent to what the legacy system creates.

scenarios.name: close-case-no-contact
scenarios.description:
Post-call writeback for the NO-CONTACT / Unconfirmed branch.

Mirrors the legacy post-call logging flow — the negative
(outcome != "completed") path:

  PATCH /sobjects/Case/{id} -> flip Case to Closed with
  Resolution_Code__c = "RC: Unconfirmed" and
  Case_Resolution_Detail_v2__c = "RC: No Contact".

No VoiceCall record is created in this branch — only calls that reach
a person get a VC record. If/when we adopt the "No Contact Case
Closure & Email" enrichment (Comments + OwnerId reassignment +
agent-routed email), extend this atom; the optional inputs
``agent_notes`` and ``agent_id`` are reserved for that.

scenarios.name: contact-for-account
scenarios.description:
Fetch the first Contact with a phone number for a given AccountId.
This is the per-account lookup the agent does after the eligibility
gate passes — it gives the dialer a real phone number.

scenarios.name: soql-open-cases
scenarios.description:
Fetch open ResCon Cases via SOQL that encodes the full pre-call contract
in code.

Filters (intersect of the customer's production ``ResCon_today`` list view
AND operational guidance):

  1. Reason = 'RC: ResCon'                         — ResCon workflow only
     (standard Case picklist; UI label "Case Reason"; NOT a __c field)
  2. Status IN ('Active','Open','Re-Opened')       — excludes Closed
  3. Total_Amount_Due__c <= 50 OR NULL             — Scribe $50 gate
  4. Subject NOT LIKE '%Will Use My Week, ...%'    — excludes WUMW records
  5. Waitlist_Check_in_Date__c >= TODAY            — past dates should be
                                                     skipped because those
                                                     reservations have
                                                     already been missed.

Filters 1-4 mirror the 6-filter set on the ``ResCon_today`` saved list
view. Filter 5 is NOT on that view — past dates should be skipped but
the SF view owner hasn't added it. Enforcing it here keeps us correct
regardless of list-view drift.

Phase 1 scope: only Accounts with exactly one future open date are
callable. Accounts with multiple future dates are deferred to Phase 2
(chronological looping). That grouping happens in ``pre-call-pipeline``
(transform node, post-dedupe) — keeping the SOQL single-shot so the
eligibility numerator (total matching rows) is still auditable vs the
final dial count.
